terms.definition: A program type with a drawing canvas and animation utilities like pause().
terms.example: public class MyArt extends GraphicsProgram { public void run() { /* draw here */ } }

terms.definition: Base class for all drawable objects (shapes, images, labels) on the canvas.
A rectangle graphic with width and height.
terms.example: GRect box = new GRect(80, 40); box.setFilled(true); add(box, 100, 100);

terms.definition: An ellipse/circle defined by width and height.
terms.example: GOval ball = new GOval(30, 30); ball.setFilled(true); add(ball, 200, 150);

terms.definition: A straight line between two points.
An image loaded from a file.
A group of GObjects that move together as one.
terms.example: GCompound ship = new GCompound(); ship.add(new GRect(40, 10), 0, 0); add(ship, 100, 300);

terms.definition: Controls whether a shape is filled and its fill color.
Shifts an object by (dx, dy) relative to its current position.
Places an object at an absolute (x, y) position.
Delays the program for a number of milliseconds, enabling smooth animation.
Returns the topmost GObject at a canvas point, or null if none.
The drawing order (front-to-back layering) of objects on the canvas.
Mouse and keyboard handlers that respond to user input.
